title: design:export silently swallows failures from the first npx command
reasoning: The design:export script chains two npx commands with &&. If the first export (--format tailwind) fails, the second (--format dtcg) is never run, but no explicit error handling or exit-code propagation exists beyond what && provides. More critically, npx downloads and caches @google/design.md on each run if not locally installed, making build reproducibility dependent on network availability and npm registry state. A CI pipeline invoking this script may succeed with only a partial export without any visible failure unless set -e or equivalent is active in the shell environment.
recommendation: Either add @google/design.md as a devDependency for reproducible installs, or wrap the npx calls with explicit error handling (e.g., set -e; npx ... && npx ...). Consider running both exports unconditionally or capturing exit codes.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No post-export validation step checks that both output files were produced and contain valid content.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a script that asserts both design-export.tailwind.json and design-export.dtcg.json exist and are non-empty after design:export runs.
minimumFixScope: Add error handling to the design:export script or make @google/design.md a declared dependency.
